one_line_takeaway: ReasonMark leaves a reasoning LLM's chain-of-thought untouched and instead derives a semantic 'compass' (PSV) from it to modulate KGW-style green-list logit bonuses during answer generation, simultaneously beating prior watermarks on text quality/task accuracy, detection AUC, and robustness to paraphrase/translation attacks on C4, WMT16, AIME, and GSM8K with Qwen3-32B and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B.
key_finding: On C4, ReasonMark achieves the lowest PPL among all watermarked methods (10.31 for Qwen3-32B, 10.54 for DeepSeek, vs. 10.55/10.82 unwatermarked) with the highest AUC (99.31 and 99.52 respectively). On WMT16-DE-EN it obtains the best BLEU among watermarking techniques (9.916 Qwen3, 9.653 DeepSeek) at AUC 87.25/85.10. On AIME and GSM8K it attains the highest mathematical accuracy among watermarked methods and, on AIME/DeepSeek, mACC of 71.34 essentially matches the no-watermark baseline (71.52), while AUC exceeds 99.9 on AIME. Robustness (Fig. 2, C4/Qwen3): unattacked AUC 0.9931, staying above 0.935 under word-delete/insert/synonym-replace, and retaining AUC 0.8259 under translation round-trip and 0.7054 under paraphrase attack -- the best among all compared methods (e.g., KGW drops to 0.8128/0.7124 under the same attacks). Ablation (Table 2, C4/Qwen3): full ReasonMark PPL 10.3080/AUC 99.31; removing Critical Token selection (w/o CTs, replaced by random tokens) is the most damaging, PPL rises to 12.8801; removing GCC raises PPL to 11.1510 (quality-focused); removing CPS drops AUC to 98.69 (detectability-focused) -- indicating the two Criticality-Score components serve complementary roles. Hyperparameter sweep: PPL follows a U-shaped curve in the PSV update rate beta_0 (best near 0.1) and in top-k (best around k=10), and can even slightly beat the no-watermark PPL baseline at tuned settings.
core_contribution: ReasonMark, a watermarking framework for reasoning LLMs (RLLMs) that decouples generation into an undisturbed 'Thinking Phase' and a watermarked 'Answering Phase'. It defines a Criticality Score (CS = GCC(w) * log(1 + CPS(w)), combining a Global Causal Contribution term and a Competitive Persistence Scoring term) to select Top-K Critical Tokens from the model's chain-of-thought, distills their embeddings via PCA into a continuous Principal Semantic Vector (PSV, the first principal component of the critical-token embedding matrix). During the Answering Phase, the standard KGW green/red-list logit-bias watermark is made semantically adaptive: each green-list token's bonus delta_i,w = delta_0 + delta_lambda * cosine_similarity(token_embedding, PSV) is scaled by its cosine alignment with the current PSV, and the PSV itself is updated after each generated answer token via an adaptive exponential moving average (rate beta_i = beta_base * cosine_similarity of the newly generated token). Detection is unchanged from KGW's standard stateless green-token z-test.
threat_model: Content-provenance / IP-protection setting, not an adversarial-attack paper against models. The paper treats LLM providers as the watermark embedder wanting to invisibly mark reasoning-model outputs for later detection (traceability of AI-generated text, mitigating misuse of RLLM outputs), while an adversary attempts to strip or evade the watermark via text perturbation. Two attack classes are evaluated: A1, word-level perturbations (insertion/deletion/synonym substitution on ~30% of tokens) and A2, semantic-level transformations (round-trip translation via DeepSeek-V3 API, and paraphrasing). No adaptive/white-box watermark-removal attacks, no model-extraction or jailbreak threats, and no consideration of an adversary who knows the PSV mechanism and specifically targets it.
limitations: The paper's own text does not report a study of an adversary aware of the PSV-guided scheme itself (i.e., no attack specifically designed to disrupt the Criticality-Score/PSV mechanism) -- robustness is only tested against generic text-perturbation attacks (A1/A2) that are agnostic to the watermark's semantic design. All main experiments use only two 32B-scale open models (Qwen3-32B, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B); generalization to other model families/sizes is not shown in the main text. The Causal Divergence term in the theoretical Criticality Score (Theorem 2.2) is stated as computationally prohibitive (requires gradients over all model parameters) and is only approximated via the GCC heuristic, so the empirical algorithm is a proxy rather than a direct instantiation of the theorem; no formal bound on this approximation gap is given in the sections reviewed. Latency claims ('negligible increase') are asserted with results deferred to an appendix not reviewed here rather than shown in the main experiments section.

quantitative_results: Robustness (C4/Qwen3, Fig. 2): ReasonMark AUC unattacked 0.9931, word-delete 0.9436-ish region (>0.935 across word attacks), translation attack 0.8259, paraphrase attack 0.7054; KGW comparably drops to translation 0.8128, paraphrase 0.7124
Ablation (Table 2, C4/Qwen3): full model PPL 10.3080/AUC 99.31; w/o CTs PPL 12.8801/AUC 99.21; w/o GCC PPL 11.1510/AUC 99.11; w/o CPS PPL 11.0597/AUC 98.69
Averaged improvement over baselines cited in abstract/intro: PPL reduced by 0.35, BLEU increased by 0.164, mathematical accuracy raised by 0.67 points, detection AUC improved by 0.34%
